rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs For Collection Of Judgment [C.C.P. 685.040, 685.080] Waiver Of Fees Under Government Code Section 6103(A) [And Court Repoter)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 24, 2021, Line 1. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs For Collection Of Judgment [C.C.P. 685.040, 685.080] Waiver Of Fees Under Government Code Section 6103(A) [And Court Repoter) is GRANTED as requested. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/VP)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 24, 2021, Line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is DENIED. Venue is proper in San Francisco under CCP 394, 395 and 397. Moving party failed to cite binding applicable authority for the proposition that enforcement of judgment is a separate proceeding from the underlying action giving rise to the claim that venue is no longer proper as Defendants' claimed residency changed since 2015. Venue is determined at the commencement of the action. Defendants failed to carry their burden under CCP 397. Plaintiff's request for fees is DENIED. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Zoom appearance is preferred. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/VP)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA MARTINEZ ET AL VS. OCTAVIO TIJERINO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 24, 2021, Line 3. DEFENDANT OCTAVIO TIJERINO, MARITZA TIJERINO'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is DENIED, moot. The basis for withdrawal motion filed on January 26, 2021 is lack of communication with client.
